heroDesc: High-resolution additive manufacturing for micro features
bodyTitle: Micro 3D Printing for microstructures, microchannels and precision prototypes
bodyDesc: Micro 3D printing is used for fine structures that are difficult for CNC or standard 3D printing, such as microchannels, hole arrays, miniature fixtures, optical features and sensor-related structures. Feasible feature size depends on machine resolution, material system, support strategy and post-cure deformation, so sample validation is recommended.

panels.title: Design Focus
panels.subtitle: Feature Size
panels.body: Fine posts, thin walls and micro holes need material and support review.

panels.title: Dimension Validation
panels.subtitle: Prototype First
panels.body: Microstructures often require microscopic inspection and functional testing.

panels.title: Post-Processing
panels.subtitle: Cleaning / Curing
panels.body: Residual resin, support removal and cure shrinkage affect detail.

tableRows: Strength and heat resistance depend on material

steps.title: Printability review
steps.body: Check minimum features, wall thickness, supports and cleaning paths.

steps.title: Orientation and support planning
steps.body: Balance precision, surface quality and support-removal risk.

steps.title: Printing and post-cure
steps.body: Print, clean, cure and remove supports as needed.

steps.title: Microscopic inspection
steps.body: Check critical microstructures, clogging risk and function.

asideCta.title: Need a Manufacturing Plan?
asideCta.body: After drawings are submitted, engineers can review geometry, material and volume to suggest a suitable process route.

related.title: Related Process Recommendations
related.desc: Depending on geometry, material and delivery stage, other processes can be combined into a complete manufacturing flow.

items.title: Metal 3D Printing
items.body: For complex metal structures and internal channels.

items.title: Urethane Casting
items.body: Can replicate selected prototype geometry in low volume.

faq.title: Micro 3D Printing FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is Micro 3D Printing suitable for?
items.a: Micro 3D Printing is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a Micro 3D Printing quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Micro 3D Printing?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
